title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved maze trace contains one plausible coordinate, wall, graph, search, event-loop, focus, render, persistence, package, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.
Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.
Record keyboard, pointer, vision, color, motion, timing, cognition, text, and assistive-technology needs as design inputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug puzzle team handles case 6421 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; all graph claims need an independent witness; then replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Scope Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: maze-scope-transfer-10laed" that uses case 7282 to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Scope Transfer.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Scope Transfer into observable performance evidence. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Scope Transfer, keep Scope Transfer connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-scope-transfer-10laed
# Competency: maze-scope-transfer.
# Case 7282: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def evidence_maze_scope_transfer_10laed(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_10laed = "10laed-deal01-7282"
    assert evidence_variant_10laed
    evidence_axes_10laed = (
        "grid10laed",
        "topology7282",
        "searchdeal01",
        "event10laed7282",
        "access728210laed",
        "recoverydeal017282",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_10laed)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"
